Title: Large-scale multi-omics unveils host–microbiome interactions driving root development and nitrogen acquisition

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for Sphingopyxis-mediated promotion of lateral root development, biomass increase, and nitrogen acquisition, especially under low nitrogen
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential macronutrient for plant growth and yield
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TWAS/GWAS/eQTL association; qRT–PCR after inoculation; Arabidopsis orthologue mutant analysis; phenotyping of fresh weight and lateral root density
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sphingopyxis inoculation; low nitrogen and high nitrogen conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaA01g23120D was identified in the Sphingopyxis-associated host network. 2. Its expression was significantly induced by Sphingopyxis, particularly under low nitrogen. 3. Arabidopsis orthologue mutants lost the Sphingopyxis-induced increase in fresh weight and lateral root number, supporting a causal role in microbiome-mediated nitrogen acquisition.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Encoded protein was reported to have serine/threonine kinase activity and previous annotation suggests involvement in abscisic acid signal transduction
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated after Sphingopyxis inoculation, especially under low nitrogen
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA986524, PRJNA990484; 16S rRNA: PRJNA956663, PRJNA960662; metabolomics: figshare https://figshare.com/s/caa71efed449b6f8c4e0
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising host target for breeding microbiome-responsive, nitrogen-efficient rapeseed varieties

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for Sphingopyxis-mediated promotion of lateral root development, biomass accumulation, and nitrogen acquisition
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential macronutrient for plant growth and biomass production
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TWAS/GWAS/eQTL association; qRT–PCR after inoculation; Arabidopsis orthologue mutant analysis; phenotyping of fresh weight and lateral root density
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sphingopyxis inoculation; low nitrogen and high nitrogen conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC07g46780D was identified as a Sphingopyxis-associated host gene. 2. Its expression increased after Sphingopyxis inoculation, especially under low nitrogen. 3. Mutation of the Arabidopsis orthologue abolished the bacterium-induced increase in fresh weight and lateral root number, indicating a causal role in microbiome-enabled nitrogen acquisition.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous to Arabidopsis AT4G37800; linked to carbon metabolism and cell wall organization
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated after Sphingopyxis inoculation, especially under low nitrogen
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA986524, PRJNA990484; 16S rRNA: PRJNA956663, PRJNA960662; metabolomics: figshare https://figshare.com/s/caa71efed449b6f8c4e0
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate host gene for microbiome-assisted breeding to improve nitrogen uptake under nutrient limitation

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: γ-glutamyl phosphate / glutamate-5-semialdehyde (via bifunctional reaction), leading to delta1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the rate-limiting conversion of glutamate toward delta1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate in proline biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potentially contributes to amino acid metabolism linked to Sphingopyxis-associated host responses; no direct metabolite phenotype functionally validated in this paper
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies terminal product directly through biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Rate-limiting enzyme reported by orthology annotation in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaA05g05760D was identified among six ASV723-associated host genes. 2. It is annotated as an orthologue of Arabidopsis P5CS1, the rate-limiting enzyme in proline biosynthesis. 3. The paper did not provide direct functional validation linking this gene to proline accumulation or nitrogen acquisition.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TWAS/GWAS/eQTL association and orthology-based functional annotation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown plants at two sites (KF and YL); association with Sphingopyxis-related ASV723
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as a candidate associated gene, not experimentally validated as causal in metabolite change in this study
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 175 diverse accessions; candidate validation context included YL29 and YL260
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA986524, PRJNA990484; 16S rRNA: PRJNA956663, PRJNA960662
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Compatible solute and stress-response amino acid
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential candidate for engineering stress-associated nitrogen efficiency traits, but direct validation is lacking in this study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root tissues (inferred general site for proline-related stress metabolism)

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potentially participates in host regulatory responses associated with Sphingopyxis and nitrogen-related metabolism; no direct causal validation provided
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Nitrogen-related metabolism and host response to microbiome association
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC09g12040D was one of six genes associated with ASV723. 2. It encodes a Dof zinc finger transcription factor. 3. No direct downstream targets or functional validation for nutrient phenotype were shown in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field conditions at KF and YL; association with Sphingopyxis-related ASV723
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Sphingopyxis-associated host response; low nitrogen context inferred
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for microbiome-responsive nutrient efficiency, pending validation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 175 diverse accessions; validation context YL29 and YL260
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA986524, PRJNA990484; 16S rRNA: PRJNA956663, PRJNA960662
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant nutrient homeostasis and growth through regulatory control (general inference)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate gene only; not among the two genes functionally validated by qPCR/mutant tests
